title: Error on normal comment starting with Verilator
body:
I use multiple simulators in my project, including Verilator. It's common to leave comments about simulator-specific behavior. For example:

```systemverilog
// Verilator does not support class in generate block yet
`ifndef VERILATOR
  // ...
`endif
```

In this case, Verilator reports an error:

```
Unknown Verilator comment: '/*Verilator does not support class in generate block yet*/'
```

This comment is not intended as a directive, just a note for developers. But because it starts with "Verilator", it gets picked up by Verilator's directive parser and results in an error.

I've run into this several times and had to rephrase the comment to work around it. That feels a bit off, since a regular comment shouldn't have any effect on how the tool behaves.

I'd like to suggest:

1. Treat unknown Verilator comments as warnings instead of errors, so users can choose to ignore them
2. Only treat comments as directives if they start with `verilator <directive>` and the directive is recognized

Would love to hear what you think.
